name: Off-by-one Error
description: A product calculates or uses an incorrect maximum or minimum value that is 1 more or 1 less than the correct value. This commonly leads to buffer overflows, incorrect loop bounds, or improper null termination when the off-by-one value is used for memory operations.
detection_pattern: Look for TRANSFORM nodes computing loop bounds, buffer sizes, or array indices where the calculation is off by one (using <= instead of <, or forgetting null terminator space), allowing writes past STORAGE boundaries. The missing mediator is correct boundary calculation with proper fence-post accounting.
